001_CFC_ESSENCEWHO IS TALKINGCHAPTER 01
01BRAND ESSENCE

A club without a league.

Complete FC is a creative house built like a football club: a design studio, a kit atelier, a content team and a family, all wearing the same crest. We don't need a league. We live between the pitch, the streets, the screen and the fam. Reachable from the terraces, aspirational from the outside.

WE OWN FOOTBALL OFF THE PITCH:
01THE RITUALS
02THE CHARACTERS
03THE SHIRTS
04THE CLIPS
05THE JOKES
06THE ARMBAND
07THE PEOPLE WHO STILL PLAY LIKE IT MATTERS
01.1LORE · THE STARTING SEVEN

Founded 2024 by seven creatives from seven nations · film, copy, sound, design, photography, content and one who reads the game like a manager. Every list in this brand is a lineup. The Seven wear numbers 1-7, forever.

01UNITED KINGDOM · DIRECTION (C)
02UNITED STATES · STRATEGY
03MEXICO · DESIGN
04JAMAICA · CONTENT
05LEBANON · FILM
06CHILE · SOUND
07NIGERIA · CULTURE
002_CFC_CRESTFIVE FORMATIONS · ONE BLOODLINECHAPTER 02
02CREST SYSTEM

One club. Five formations.

The responsive identity, from full ceremony to a single stitched glyph. The mark changes size, never meaning. The knight is the institution; the mastiff is the character. They meet formally only in Tier 01.

TIER 01HERITAGE CRESTDESIGN STUDY
GARTERTHE CAPTAIN'S ARMBAND
CHARGEPERIOD BALL · SEVEN CHIEF
USEEMBROIDERY · FOIL · CEREMONY
TIER 02MATCH CRESTDESIGN STUDY
ELEMENTSSHIELD + HELM + CFC + SEVEN
MIN SIZE36MM WOVEN · 24MM PRINT
USESLEEVES · PATCHES · PENNANTS
TIER 03SQUIRE HELMETCURRENT VECTOR
ROLETHE MANUFACTURER'S STAMP
MIN SIZE18PX / 8MM
USEAVATARS · CHEST HITS · BALL MARKS
TIER 04GOTHIC CDESIGN STUDY
CONSTRUCTIONC SHELTERS THE HELM
MIN SIZE12MM SOCK STITCH
USECAPS · SOCKS · CORNER STAMPS
TIER 05WORDMARK + CODECURRENT VECTORS
STACKEDLABELS · SQUARE FORMATS
CIRCULARPRODUCT BADGE · SOCIAL
USENAVIGATION · TAPE · FOOTERS
SWIPE OR DRAG · 5 FORMATIONS

Commission 001 in full - the written brief, plus a References board (mood, style, adjacent brands) and an Elements board (the literal pieces each mark needs) for every tier. A guide, not a locked spec: work whichever tier is most interesting first.

The Crest Brief · Commission 001

FOR THE ILLUSTRATOR / DESIGNER · TIER 01 + TIER 04 ARE NEW DRAWINGS · TIER 03 IS A REDRAW
Concept

Full heraldic arms for a club that exists off the pitch. The captain's armband is drawn as the garter encircling the shield: the club's own object doing the Order-of-the-Garter job. The motto banner beneath reads "LOYAL TO THE BEAUTIFUL GAME". Seven stitch marks sit at the base: seven founders, seven nations. EST. 2024 within the garter.

Cast + hierarchy
  • Knight helm: the institution. Crowns the shield, faces dexter (left).
  • English Mastiff: the character. Single supporter beside the shield, calm, seated or standing guard. Never cartoon, never aggressive.
  • Shield charge: a period football (18-panel or T-panel) or the helm, to be explored.
  • Laurel: half-wreath under the shield, tucked behind the banner.
Technique
  • Engraving / etching linework: hatching, not gradients. Reference: banknote lions, the three-headed-wolf W, Burberry equestrian knight redraw.
  • Must survive one colour. Test in Goal Black on Chalk and Chalk on Claret before any detail pass.
  • Embroidery test at 60mm wide: if the mastiff's face dies at 60mm, simplify the drawing, not the concept.
  • Gold exists only as finish: foil, thread, deboss. Never flat digital gold.
System obligations
  • Tier 01 must reduce visibly into Tier 02: same shield geometry, same helm angle.
  • Tier 03 redraw keeps today's silhouette recognisable (avatars must not break) but adopts the new line logic.
  • Tier 04 Gothic C: custom blackletter, mastiff head or full body woven through the counterspace. Must hold as a 12mm sock stitch.
  • The crest must sit naturally in: the centre circle seal, the ticket stub, the armband, a wax seal, a pitch-side flag.
Deliverables
  • Vector masters (AI/SVG) for all five tiers, one-colour + two-colour cuts.
  • Clear-space + minimum sizes per tier.
  • Embroidery-ready simplification of Tiers 01/02/04.
  • One lockup sheet: five tiers on Tunnel Green, Claret, Chalk.
Tier
01
Tier 01 - Heritage Crest

Heritage Crest

The most formal register in the system. Full heraldry - the crest built as a coat of arms, in the company of Burberry's knight, Hermes' carriage, the old European shields.

References
Elements

Mastiff, captain band, shield if needed, football elements, knight armor elements, crown?

Treat the reference sheet as ingredients, not an assembly order.

Tier
02
Tier 02 - Match Crest

Match Crest

Same DNA as Tier 01, considerably less ornament. This is the badge that gets embroidered, stamped small on a corner flag, printed at 20mm and still reads.

References
Elements

Simplified Mastiff, football ball, COMPLETE FC, crown if needed.

The test for this tier isn't beauty at full size - it's legibility at match size.

Tier
03
Tier 03 - Helmet

Helmet

One symbol, pulled out of the armor language and given its own life - closer to a monogram or a hallmark than a crest.

References
Elements

Knight helmet.

This is the mark most likely to work small: app icons, hang tags, a single stitched patch.

Tier
04
Tier 04 - Alternative Wordmark

Alternative Wordmark

Gothic, blackletter, closer to a vintage varsity patch or a streetwear back-print than the club crest. This is the wordmark for drop pieces, not the badge.

References
Elements

COMPLETE FC + Letter C.

Reference points are directional, not a style to copy edge for edge.

Tier
05
Tier 05 - Wordmark + Emblem Update

Wordmark + Emblem

The current wordmark stays as is. What's open is the emblem sitting beside it, refined once a direction lands from Tiers 01 through 04.

References - Current Lockup

Current wordmark (fixed) + emblem (open - vector files to follow separately).

003_CFC_TYPOGRAPHYTHREE VOICESCHAPTER 03
03TYPOGRAPHY

Three voices. No system fonts.

Every composition speaks with the Institution, is catalogued by the Archive, and is rarely blessed by the Ceremony. Faces shown are stand-ins; licensing targets per voice below. This chapter is a directional guide, not a locked type spec.

VOICE 01 · THE INSTITUTIONHeadlines · wordmark · numbers
Stand-in: Archivo condensed black
License: Right Grotesk / Monument Grotesk / Founders X-Cond
Built different.
Catalogued forever.
VOICE 02 · THE CEREMONYOne word per composition · back prints · mottos
Stand-in: Pirata One
License: F37 Bergman / Fette Fraktur / custom Gothic C
Skills + Soul
VOICE 03 · THE ARCHIVEFile names · spec lists · captions · labels
Stand-in: Space Mono
License: Sohne Mono / NB International Mono
POSITION WINGER SQUAD NO. 07 DROP CHAPTER 02 · SS26 EDITION 200 UNITS · NOT FOR RESALE
004_CFC_COLOURTWO FIELDS · FIVE NEUTRALS · TWO ACCENTSCHAPTER 04
04COLOUR · PROPORTIONS

Two fields carry the club.
Accents earn their minutes.

Tunnel Green and Claret are the home and away worlds: deep, elegant, built for gold thread and chalk type. Neutrals do the everyday work. Red Card is the primary accent, the referee moment. Pitch Volt is the super-sub: rare by law, so that when it appears, it means something.

TUNNEL GREEN 30
CLARET 20
CHALK 14
SAND 12
STONE 6
GRAPHITE 8
BLACK 6
3
FIELDS 50 · NEUTRALS 46ACCENTS 4 · ONE PER COMPOSITION

CLARETAway world · heritage20 · #380B16

#D1C2AB

#C7C2B6

RED CARDPrimary · ceremony heat3 · #E5261F
PITCH VOLTSecondary · the super-sub1 · #AEFE15

Rule 01 · Fields, not backgrounds

Green and Claret are worlds with their own drops, photography and objects. Home and away. Neither is ever a small highlight.

Rule 02 · Volt is rationed by law

Pitch Volt appears in at most one element per composition, and never in two consecutive artefacts. Scarcity is the point: it is the 89th-minute substitution.

Rule 03 · Gold is a finish

Trophy Gold (#C9A24B) exists as foil, thread and deboss on heritage objects only. It never enters digital layouts as flat colour.

005_CFC_SYSTEMEVERY LAYOUT IS A PITCHCHAPTER 05
05DESIGN SYSTEM

Layouts that could only
come from football.

The system is built from the sport's own furniture: pitch markings, the armband, the ticket, the team sheet, the scoreline. Rationing rule: maximum two devices per composition. The devices are seasoning, not costume.

C
THE TUNNEL · file bar, always on top
CORNER ARCS · the frame's signature
HALFWAY LINE · section divider
CENTRE CIRCLE · seal / crest position
TOUCHLINE · chalk border, 2px minimum
THE DUGOUT · captions + credits

Device 01 · Touchline Frame

Chalk borders with corner arcs, halfway-line dividers, centre-circle seals. The quarter-circle corner is the one-glance tell.

Device 02 · The Armband

A stitched band wrapping the layout like a sleeve: slightly rotated, edge to edge, carrying the Gothic C and one line. The captain moment of a composition.

Device 03 · The Fixture

Everything announced like a match. Drops have kickoffs. CFC vs the world. Competition line above, kickoff meta below.

Device 04 · The Ticket

Drop info as a match ticket: gate, row, seat become channel, chapter, edition. Perforated stub doubles as hang tag.

Device 05 · The Team Sheet

Every list is a lineup with shirt numbers and a captain's (C). Products, credits, founders: all starting elevens.

Device 06 · The Scoreline

Statements as results. Full-time captions, goalscorer lines with minutes. Trash talk with a straight face.

007_CFC_PHOTOGRAPHYSHOT ON GRASSCHAPTER 07
07PHOTOGRAPHY

Football, human again.

Grass, floodlights, sweat, fathers, sons, friends, boots, hands, armbands and imperfect moments. Film texture over studio gloss. Motion blur is welcome; AI gloss is banned. If it looks like a sports-drink ad, reshoot.

DO · REAL PITCHES, REAL LIGHT, REAL PEOPLE
DO · GRAIN, FLASH, BLUR, IMPERFECTION
DO · DETAILS: BOOTS, HANDS, STITCHING, TURF
DON'T · STUDIO SEAMLESS, DUOTONE GRADIENTS
DON'T · AI-GENERATED PLAYERS
DON'T · STOCK CELEBRATION CLICHES
008_CFC_VOICETERRACE RULESCHAPTER 08
08VOICE

Talk like the terraces.
Dress like the tunnel.

Short. Certain. Football-literate. We never explain the joke and never beg for the follow. Aspiration comes from acting like the club already exists · because it does.

EXCLUSIVITY
drops · editions
NO NEUTRALS.
SELL-OUT
post-drop
SOLD OUT IS A SCORELINE.
BELONGING
the fam
FROM US. FOR THE FAM. Forever.
CALENDAR
seasons
WE DON'T DO SEASONS.
WE DO CHAPTERS.
SIGN-OFF
everything
Loyal to the Beautiful Game.
BANNED: "COMMUNITY-DRIVEN" · "ELEVATED ESSENTIALS" · "LIMITED EDITION" (SAY: EDITION 200, THEN FULL TIME) · "DON'T MISS OUT" · EXPLAINING THE JOKE · APOLOGISING FOR THE PRICE
009_CFC_DROPSCHAPTERS, NOT SEASONSCHAPTER 09
09DROP ARCHITECTURE

Every drop is a chapter.

Low volume, high intention, no random merch. Each chapter has a world (Home = Tunnel Green, Away = Claret), a fixture date, an edition count and one hero object. Sold out is full time; there is no restock, only the next fixture.

CH 01HOME · TUNNEL GREEN · MATCH JERSEY + ARMBANDEDITION 200 · FULL TIME
CH 02AWAY · CLARET · HERITAGE KNIT + TICKET STUB TAGSEDITION 200 · KICKOFF 10.07
CH 03THIRD · KIT SAND · SUNDAY LEAGUE CAPSULEEDITION 300 · TBC
SPHERITAGE OBJECT · GOLD FINISH · HANDMADE BALL / PENNANTEDITION 50 · CEREMONY
010_CFC_RULESTHE LAWS OF THE GAMECHAPTER 10
10DO / DON'T

The laws of the game.

DO

  • Lead with the fields: green home, claret away.
  • Max two devices per composition.
  • One gothic word per layout, maximum size.
  • File bar on top, stamp at the bottom, always.
  • Write fixtures, tickets, lineups, scorelines.
  • Let photography breathe inside the touchline frame.
  • Gold as thread and foil on heritage objects.

DON'T

  • Volt in more than one element, ever.
  • Volt and Red Card in the same composition.
  • Blackletter as body copy or in paragraphs.
  • Centered startup-hero layouts with no furniture.
  • Gradient duotones on photography.
  • "Limited edition", "don't miss out", explaining the joke.
  • Flat gold in digital layouts.
011_CFC_HANDOFFFOR THE DESIGNERCHAPTER 11
11DESIGNER HANDOFF

Take it further.

This document is the base, not the ceiling. The narrative, palette weights, devices and briefs are fixed intent; the craft is yours to push. Priority order below.

Commission first
  • Tier 01 Heritage Crest + Tier 04 Gothic C (see Crest Brief, Ch. 02).
  • Tier 03 Squire Helmet redraw in the new line DNA.
  • Custom or licensed Institution face; retire all system fonts.
Then build
  • Master grid spec from the pitch anatomy (Ch. 05) for 1:1, 4:5, 9:16, 16:9 and A-series print.
  • Armband, ticket and team-sheet as reusable templates.
  • Chapter 02 drop kit: fixture, ticket, lookbook, scoreline, hang tags.
Licensing targets
  • Institution: Right Grotesk / Monument Grotesk / Founders X-Cond.
  • Ceremony: F37 Bergman / Fette Fraktur, evolving into the custom Gothic C.
  • Archive: Sohne Mono / NB International Mono.
Open questions for the room
  • Shield charge: period football or helm? Explore both.
  • Claret vs green split per chapter: fixed or alternating?
  • The Seven: named publicly or kept as numbers?
